pi_items.source: Verified against Cleveland Clinic pediatric vital signs reference ranges (https://health.clevelandclinic.org/pediatric-vital-signs). Normal heart rate for 1-3 year old: 80-125 bpm; 3-6 years: 70-115 bpm; 6-12 years: 60-100 bpm. Value of 125 bpm is at the upper limit of normal for toddlers (1-3 years) and above normal for older children, consistent with a pediatric simulation profile for younger children.

Verified against Cleveland Clinic pediatric vital signs reference ranges (https://health.clevelandclinic.org/pediatric-vital-signs). Normal respiratory rate for 1-3 year old: 20-30 bpm; 3-6 years: 20-25 bpm; 6-12 years: 14-22 bpm. Value of 28 bpm is within the normal range for toddlers (1-3 years) but above normal for older children, consistent with pediatric simulation for younger children.

Verified against pediatric SpO2 standards from multiple sources. Traditional reference range: 95-100%. Current evidence-based range: 97-100%. Value of 96% falls within traditional range (95-100%) and is clinically reasonable for pediatric patients, though slightly below modern recommendations (97-100%). Confirmed in multiple peer-reviewed sources on normal pulse oximetry values in children (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12010496/).

Verified as clinically reasonable pediatric infusion rate. Research shows pediatric infusions commonly use rates ranging from 0.4-1.0 mL/hr for microdose (syringe pump) to standard rates depending on medication and patient weight. 10 mL/hr is a typical maintenance or moderate infusion rate for pediatric patients. Confirmed in pediatric infusion standards and fluid management literature (https://www.ashp.org/-/media/assets/pharmacy-practice/s4s/docs/Pediatric-Infusion-Standards.pdf).

Confirmed in task.json description ('Write a configuration report to /home/ga/Desktop/pediatric_config.txt'), setup_task.sh line 17 (rm -f /home/ga/Desktop/pediatric_config.txt), and export_result.sh line 14 (REPORT_PATH=/home/ga/Desktop/pediatric_config.txt). This is the consistent report file path used throughout the task for documenting device configuration.

Confirmed in task.json description ('take a screenshot of the app and save it to /home/ga/Desktop/pediatric_evidence.png'), setup_task.sh line 18 (rm -f /home/ga/Desktop/pediatric_evidence.png), and export_result.sh line 15 (EVIDENCE_PATH=/home/ga/Desktop/pediatric_evidence.png). This is the consistent screenshot file path used throughout the task for evidence documentation.

privileged_info_summary: The pediatric_simulation_profile task is a validated clinical simulation scenario using OpenICE (https://www.openice.info/), a real open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP Program at Massachusetts General Hospital. The task requires creating two simulated medical device adapters: (1) a Multiparameter Monitor with pediatric vital signs configuration (HR 125 bpm, RR 28 bpm, SpO2 96%), and (2) an Infusion Pump set to 10 mL/hr. All vital sign values are clinically reasonable and within or near established pediatric reference ranges: HR 125 bpm is at the upper limit of normal for 1-3 year olds; RR 28 bpm is within normal range for toddlers; SpO2 96% is within traditional reference range (95-100%). The infusion rate of 10 mL/hr is a typical pediatric infusion rate. All devices and their vital signs data are synthetic, generated by OpenICE's simulation framework, not real patient data. The task requires creating a configuration report at /home/ga/Desktop/pediatric_config.txt and an evidence screenshot at /home/ga/Desktop/pediatric_evidence.png. All metadata claims have been independently verified against pediatric vital signs standards, infusion guidelines, task.json description, setup scripts, and official OpenICE documentation.
